title: Godoya disticha
taxonomy: Godoya disticha is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. It belongs to the genus Godoya, which is native to Mexico. The classification of Godoya disticha places it within the order Ebenales and the clade Sapindanae. This plant is also sometimes referred to as a member of the family Ebenaceae, although this is considered a synonym of Sapotaceae. Further study is needed to confirm the exact classification of Godoya disticha.

intro: Godoya disticha, also known as the Double-Leaf Godoya, is a species of plant in the Sapindaceae family, native to Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Godoya disticha include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Godoya disticha is commonly prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, and is often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders.
